verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is canonically a secreted serine protease abundantly released into prostatic fluid with no TM domain. However, recent studies (Shen et al., Clin Cancer Res 2025) confirmed cell surface expression on prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP, FACS + confocal co-localization with EpCAM) and patient-derived mCRPC tumor cells. This surface pool is restricted to prostate cancer / AR+ oncogenic state, not observed on normal cells at baseline, and has enabled T-cell engagers, CAR-T, RLT, and radioligand clinical programs (e.g., Pasritamig/JNJ-78278343). The surface display is thus cancer-cell/oncogenic-state induced, qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced.
